novel_features.name: Superlative & keyword categories
novel_features.description: Find the biggest, smallest, tallest, or weird-food attractions (and more) by classifying cached attractions locally.
novel_features.rationale: The site's theme slugs are internal and undocumented; we classify by name/description patterns over the local cache, which the website offers no command for.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Reach for this when the user wants superlatives or a themed slice (giants, muffler-men, weird-food) rather than a place- or state-scoped list.

novel_features.name: Quirk leaderboard / rollups
novel_features.description: Summarize the local cache: counts by state and by category, plus totals.
novel_features.rationale: Requires aggregation across cached attractions that the site never exposes.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use to understand coverage of the local cache before planning, or to answer 'which state has the most offbeat stuff cached'.

novel_features.name: Surprise-me
novel_features.description: Pick a random offbeat attraction, optionally constrained by state or category.
novel_features.rationale: Weighted random selection over the local cache with structured output; the site's 'roll the dice' has no machine-readable form.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use for serendipity or road-trip inspiration when the user has no specific target.

novel_features.name: Road-trip stops
novel_features.description: Collect quirky stops near a list of cities or coordinates in one call, deduped and labeled by stop.
novel_features.rationale: Aggregates nearby results across multiple geocoded stops, turning the catalog into a route-planning primitive no UI offers.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Reach for this when planning a route and the user wants offbeat stops across several waypoints at once.

novel_features.name: State quirk comparison
novel_features.description: Compare two states by offbeat-attraction count and surface a few top picks from each.
novel_features.rationale: Cross-state counts and top picks in a single local join; the site has no compare view.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use when the user is deciding between regions or wants a quick 'which state is weirder' answer.

narrative.headline: Every offbeat roadside attraction on RoadsideAmerica.com — with a local SQLite cache, offline search, and superlative categories no other tool offers.
narrative.value_prop: RoadsideAmerica.com is the web's best catalog of quirky US & Canada tourist attractions, but it has no API and a paywalled app. This CLI turns it into an agent-native, pipe-friendly tool: find what's near a place or coordinates, browse a whole state, pull the full writeup, and slice by superlative categories like biggest/smallest/tallest/weird-food. Everything is cached locally (fresh-on-read), every record links back to its source, and the scraper stays a polite, attributing, user-initiated citizen of the site.

quickstart.comment: health + reachability check; no auth needed
quirky attractions near a city (geocoded), within 20 miles
browse offbeat attractions in a state
full writeup + location + source link for one attraction

troubleshoots.fix: The CLI self-limits to ~1 request/3s and caches in SQLite; let it back off, or rely on cached data (results show fetched_at).
Widen --radius, or pass coordinates directly: near 30.27,-97.74 --radius 25.
Pass latitude,longitude directly to near/trip; geocoding uses keyless OSM Nominatim which may rate-limit.
Categories classify the LOCAL cache; run state/sync/near first to populate, then re-run category.

narrative.when_to_use: Use this CLI to discover offbeat, quirky, community-sourced roadside attractions in the US and Canada from RoadsideAmerica.com: what's near a place or coordinates, what's in a given state, the full writeup for a specific attraction, or superlative slices like the biggest/smallest/tallest things and weird food stops. It is ideal for road-trip planning and 'find me something weird nearby' tasks, and it caches everything locally for fast, repeatable, offline-friendly queries.
anti_triggers: Do not use for booking, ticketing, hours, or live availability — the data is descriptive, community-sourced, and not real-time.
Do not treat addresses as authoritative for navigation; verify before driving.
Do not use outside the US and Canada (the source only covers those).
Do not use for mainstream tourism, restaurant reviews, or hotels — this is offbeat oddities only.

recipes.title: Quirky stops near coordinates, agent-friendly fields
recipes.explanation: Pass raw lat,lng to skip geocoding and select only the fields an agent needs.

recipes.title: Weird food across a cached state
recipes.explanation: Classifies cached attractions by food keywords; populate the cache with state/near first.

recipes.title: Plan offbeat stops across a route
recipes.explanation: Aggregates nearby attractions for each waypoint, deduped and labeled by stop.

recipes.title: Full writeup with source attribution
recipes.explanation: Returns structured name/address/writeup plus the RoadsideAmerica.com source URL.

trigger_phrases: offbeat roadside attractions for my road trip
biggest tourist traps in a state
